cards.title: Creativity Thrives When You Have Kids
cards.body: A parent discovers that having children actually enhances creative thinking rather than stifling it. Small moments—like a child comparing kale stalks to giraffes—spark unexpected wonder and imagination.

cards.title: Teaching Kids About Death With Courage
cards.body: Including young people in end-of-life conversations builds their emotional resilience and understanding. Sparing youth from life's universal realities only delays their struggle to reconcile with mortality.

cards.title: Has Gentle Parenting Gone Too Far?
cards.body: Recent parenting trends in the US and France prioritize gentleness and positivity, but research suggests this approach isn't always beneficial for child development. Balance between nurturing and appropriate boundaries matters.

cards.title: Perfectionism Undermines Children's Creativity
cards.body: Perfectionism increases anxiety and reduces a child's ability to be creative and imaginative. A strong self-concept and healthy risk-taking are what truly foster innovation in children.

cards.title: Why Autistic Children Struggle to Launch
cards.body: Autistic emerging adults face barriers like skills gaps and capacity limits—not lack of motivation. Understanding which specific obstacle affects your child helps determine the right support strategy.

cards.title: Children's Innate Curiosity About Life
cards.body: Kids naturally bring big questions about sex and death to conversations, seeking to make sense of the world. Therapists note that children are equipped with innate curiosity that guides these inquiries.

cards.title: YouTube Still Recommending Harmful Content to Teens
cards.body: Despite new safety rules, the platform continues recommending eating disorder videos to teenagers. Parents should monitor teen social media use and discuss potentially harmful content.

cards.title: Over Half of Adults With Learning Disabilities Die by 65
cards.body: A new report reveals stark health disparities for people with learning disabilities, with most not surviving past age 65. Experts say these findings demand urgent government attention and policy changes.

cards.title: Childcare Worker Accused of Abusing 136 Children
cards.body: An Australian daycare worker faces 329 child abuse charges spanning 16 years across five facilities. This case highlights the critical importance of thorough background checks and childcare oversight.
